message:
fix(mysql): guard against oversized packet lengths in wire protocol

Add a bounds check on the decoded packet length header before
allocating the payload buffer. MySQL's wire protocol caps packets at
16 MB - 1 byte (16,777,215). Without this check, a malformed or
malicious packet header claiming a larger size would cause an
unbounded allocation, potentially exhausting memory.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
